task_completion.description: Merge 'Brown v. Board of Education' duplicates while preserving the complete abstract.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot where 'Brown v. Board of Education' is selected in Juris-M. The right-hand metadata pane must show the 'Abstract' field containing the text 'overturning Plessy'.

task_completion.description: Merge 'Miranda v. Arizona' duplicates while preserving the complete abstract.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot where 'Miranda v. Arizona' is selected in Juris-M. The right-hand metadata pane must show the 'Abstract' field containing the text 'police custody'.

task_completion.description: Merge 'Gideon v. Wainwright' duplicates while preserving the court field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot where 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is selected in Juris-M. The right-hand metadata pane must show the 'Court' field containing the text 'Supreme Court'.

task_completion.description: Ensure no duplicate entries remain for the target cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main library list in the middle pane. There should be exactly one entry for 'Brown v. Board of Education', one for 'Miranda v. Arizona', and one for 'Gideon v. Wainwright'.

task_completion.description: Verify the final library contains exactly 10 unique items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the total number of items in the middle pane list, or look at the item count in the status bar at the bottom of the Juris-M window. There must be exactly 10 items.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Juris-M graphical interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking through the Juris-M application, selecting items, and using the UI, rather than executing terminal commands or editing a database directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the built-in merge functionality rather than manually deleting and re-typing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Merge Items' interface in the right pane when multiple duplicates are selected, showing the agent selecting the correct master item rather than manually typing metadata into a blank field.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete unrelated items to achieve the final count.
integrity.visual_evidence: Other items in the library (e.g., 'Marbury v. Madison', 'Obergefell v. Hodges', 'Tinker v. Des Moines', and law review articles) should still be visible in the middle pane list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target cases to merge: 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', and 'Gideon v. Wainwright'. The final library must contain exactly 10 items. When merging, the correct master item must be preserved: 'Brown v. Board of Education' must have an Abstract containing 'overturning Plessy'; 'Miranda v. Arizona' must have an Abstract containing 'police custody'; 'Gideon v. Wainwright' must have a Court field containing 'Supreme Court'.
